title: Canada Advances Battery Innovation with Made-in-Canada Technology
content:
The Government of Canada has announced a major investment of over $22 million to support eight innovative projects that aim to accelerate the development and production of advanced battery technologies across the country. The announcement was made in Montreal, Quebec, by The Honourable Tim Hodgson, Minister of Energy and Natural Resources. The funding supports the governmentâ€™s broader objective of strengthening Canadaâ€™s clean energy ecosystem, improving battery performance, and enhancing energy security as part of the journey toward achieving net-zero emissions by 2050.

The supported projects focus on several key areas of innovation: improving the efficiency, reliability, and affordability of energy storage technologies; reducing production costs and environmental impacts; and advancing new technologies that contribute to Canadaâ€™s energy sovereignty. These efforts are expected to enhance competitiveness, support local supply chains, and create high-quality jobs while positioning Canada as a global leader in battery technology and clean energy innovation.

Minister Hodgson highlighted that Canadaâ€™s rich critical mineral resources, combined with its scientific expertise and industrial capacity, make it ideally placed to lead in the global battery market. Supporting his remarks, Minister Steven Guilbeault emphasized that these investments are not only about energy transition but also about building a resilient economy that promotes innovation and sustainability. Parliamentary Secretary Claude Guay added that the Energy Innovation Program is fostering a network of Canadian innovators working to strengthen the nationâ€™s battery supply chain.

One of the key beneficiaries, NanoXplore, based in Saint-Laurent, will receive nearly $2.8 million to develop lithium-ion battery cells that are faster to charge, more powerful, safer, and manufactured domestically â€” advancing Canadaâ€™s commitment to clean technology and energy independence.
author: Analysts Account
